questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the DSM-5?

options.text: To prescribe specific medications for each psychological disorder
To rank psychological disorders by severity from mild to life-threatening
To determine the legal competency of individuals in court proceedings
To provide standardized criteria for diagnosing and classifying psychological disorders

questions.explanation: The DSM-5, published by the American Psychiatric Association, provides standardized diagnostic criteria that help clinicians make consistent and reliable diagnoses of psychological disorders.

questions.question: What does comorbidity mean in the context of psychological disorders?

options.text: A disorder that affects both mental and physical health equally
A disorder that is fatal if left untreated
The co-occurrence of two or more disorders in the same individual at the same time
A disorder that only occurs in combination with a medical illness

questions.explanation: Comorbidity refers to the co-occurrence of two or more disorders in the same person at the same time. For example, a person with an anxiety disorder may also experience depression. Comorbidity is common and complicates both diagnosis and treatment.
